channel.title: AI·Brief — Daily Tech Intelligence
channel.description: Automated AI-powered tech news briefings updated every 3 hours.

item.title: The James Bond of First Light Is Hot In A Grown Up Way
item.description: The latest character in First Light is captivatingly attractive, drawing significant attention. His appeal is undeniably distracting for players.

item.title: Apple just released new AirPods Pro 3 firmware, more
item.description: Apple has rolled out new firmware updates for AirPods Pro 3 and 2. Users can easily install the latest enhancements now.

item.title: Qualcomm wants to be the chip inside whatever replaces your smartphone, and it just announced two products toward that end
item.description: Qualcomm is developing over 40 AI wearable devices, including innovative jewelry and earbuds. This marks a significant shift in their product strategy.

item.title: Android 17 arrives on Pixel phones today
item.description: Pixel phones are the first to receive Android 17 along with new features. Other devices will follow later this year.

item.title: Google launches Wear OS 7 with Live Updates and a battery life boost
item.description: Wear OS 7 introduces Live Updates for deliveries and sports scores. This update enhances user experience significantly.

item.title: Destiny 2 Devs Bid Farewell To The Game They Spent A Decade Making
item.description: Bungie developers reflect on their decade-long journey with Destiny 2, highlighting both challenges and rewarding experiences.

item.title: Weeks After Launching Luna Abyss, Its Entire Development Team At Kwalee Labs Has Been Laid Off
item.description: Kwalee Labs has laid off its entire team shortly after the launch of Luna Abyss. This follows a generally positive reception of the game.

item.title: Snap unveils $2,195 AR glasses as CEO Evan Spiegel bets on post-smartphone future
item.description: Snap has launched AR glasses aimed at consumers, priced at $2,195. This move signals a shift towards augmented reality experiences.

item.title: The Steam Machine Is Likely in Testers’ Hands, as 2 Fresh SteamOS Benchmarks Leak
item.description: Steam Machine Fremont benchmarks suggest an upcoming Summer launch for Valve's new console. Testers are already using the system.

item.title: Microsoft denies killing Xbox exclusives — but the trust delta is real
item.description: Microsoft has refuted rumors about abandoning Xbox exclusives. The company emphasizes its commitment to exclusive game development.

item.title: Microsoft's new Surface Laptop and Surface Pro upgrade to Snapdragon X2 and start at $1,499
item.description: Microsoft unveils new Surface Laptop and Surface Pro models featuring Snapdragon X2. Prices start at $1,499, showcasing significant upgrades.

item.title: Schlage’s UWB-enabled smart lock launches this month
item.description: Schlage's new Sense Pro smart lock will be available starting June 29 for $399. It features advanced unlocking technology.

item.title: The preening prince of perfection
item.description: Sigvald's new Army of Renown for Warhammer Age of Sigmar allows players to dominate the battlefield with style. This adds depth to gameplay.

item.title: More Sanitation Workers Are Getting Pricked by Used Needles
item.description: Sanitation workers are increasingly encountering used needles during their duties. This raises concerns about disposal practices among users.

item.title: GTA 6 May Shatter All Sales Records with 45+ Million Units Sold on Launch Day, According to Piper Sandler
item.description: Piper Sandler predicts GTA 6 could sell over 45 million copies on launch day, potentially breaking industry sales records.
